whatMustBeOnSite: Approved plans and certification papers for fire alarm, fire protection, or fire suppression work when applicable.
Current tagged fire extinguisher and hood extinguishing system service documentation.
Monthly extinguisher visual-inspection log and annual extinguisher service proof.
Grease hauler manifests or pump logs if the site operates a grease trap or interceptor.
commonFailureReasons: Life-safety features are not in place or not functional at final inspection.
Approved plans or certification papers are missing on inspection day.
Fire extinguisher tags are expired or hood extinguishing service documentation is missing.
Excessive grease accumulation remains in the kitchen exhaust system.
rescheduleMethod: Coordinate requested fire inspections with Tampa Fire Marshal inspectors at 813-274-7000; time-specific tests or walk-throughs must be arranged directly with the inspector.
penaltyOrEscalation: Unsatisfactory re-inspections double the initial inspection fee, and a fourth re-inspection is referred to the Code Review Board.
